DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MICHELLE CURRAN, ADMINISTRATIVE DIRECTOR, JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA (SUED AS SHELLEY CURRAN) DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Michelle Curran's demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff Albert Masjedian asserts a single cause of action against Curran asking for prospective injunctive relief declaring void court orders in Los Angeles Superior Court Case No. BC593351 and California State Bar Court case number 09-0-10207/10-0-08540-PEM. The court sustains the demurrer because this court lacks the fundamental authority to enter an order voiding an order of another court. (CCP 430.10.) With respect to an order of the Los Angeles Superior Court, only a court exercising superior jurisdiction may void or overrule it. (Cf. Auto Equity Sales, Inc. v. Superior Court of Santa Clara County (1962) 57 Cal.2d 450, 455.) With respect to a State Bar Court matter, such decisions may be reviewed pursuant to Rule of Court 9.13 by petition to the Supreme Court by the licensee. A new lawsuit in this court is not an avenue of review. The court also sustains the demurrer because it fails to state a claim for relief under the Fourteenth Amendment. Plaintiff's opposing papers do not identify any facts he could allege that would cure these defects. Accordingly, the court does not grant leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
